title: Build a Paid Community from Scratch

posts._note: Talk about the problem. No link in first 3-5 lines. Make them feel the frustration. Then reveal the guide.
posts.linkedin:
You have an audience.

They follow you. They like your posts. They reply to your stories. They tell you your content helped them.

And every single month, your income resets to zero.

No retainers. No recurring revenue. Just the exhausting cycle of launching, promoting, launching again.

You know the answer is a paid community. You've thought about it for months.

But every time you try to figure out how to start, you hit the same wall:

What do I charge? What platform? How do I keep people from cancelling? How do I get the first 20 members without feeling desperate?

The truth is: most communities fail not because their creator isn't talented — but because they were built around connection instead of transformation.

Connection is a reason to hang out.
Transformation is a reason to pay.

The Revenue Community Framework™ inside the Build a Paid Community from Scratch guide gives you the exact architecture: tiered pricing, retention loops, engagement engine, and revenue multipliers — so your community generates $5,000+ MRR in 90 days and compounds from there.

→ Get the guide: https://publixion.com/guides/build-paid-community-from-scratch

#PaidCommunity #RecurringRevenue #MRR #CommunityBuilding #Publixion
posts.facebook:
You have an engaged audience. Followers, subscribers, readers who genuinely value what you create.

And yet — your income resets to zero every month.

The exhaustion of constant launching. The anxiety of inconsistent revenue. The knowledge that your audience trusts you — but you haven't built the vehicle to convert that trust into predictable income.

A paid community is that vehicle. But most creators who try to build one hit the same walls: wrong pricing, no retention system, low engagement after week one, and a churn rate that slowly drains everything they built.

The Revenue Community Framework™ is a 5-step circular system — Position, Architecture, Engagement, Retention, Revenue — that builds a $5,000+ MRR community in 90 days with scripts for every email, worksheets for every decision, and a day-by-day action calendar.

Your audience is ready. The system is what's been missing.

posts._note: Show 1 key model or system inside the guide. Build authority. Teach something real.
posts.linkedin:
82% of paid communities never reach 100 paying members.

The ones that do aren't luckier. They're structured differently.

Here's the Revenue Community Framework™ — the 5-step circular system inside the guide:

——

🎯 STEP 1 — POSITION & PROMISE
Your community must solve ONE specific, painful problem for ONE specific person. "A community for entrepreneurs" is a hangout. "A community for agency owners scaling past $20K/month" is a tool with measurable ROI.

Until you can state your transformation in one sentence — you're not ready to launch.

——

🏗️ STEP 2 — MEMBERSHIP ARCHITECTURE
Three tiers. That's it.
• Free: Lead generation pool
• Core ($15/month): Weekly Q&A, resource vault, accountability
• Premium ($49/month): 1-on-1 coaching, DM access, priority feedback

At 150 Core + 60 Premium members = $6,087 MRR. In month 3.

——

⚙️ STEP 3 — ENGAGEMENT ENGINE
A structured Mon–Fri weekly rhythm: Mini-training, Challenge, Poll, Live Q&A, Win Showcase. Members schedule their week around your community — retention becomes automatic.

——

🔄 STEP 4 — RETENTION LOOP
67% of churn happens in the first 30 days. A 4-touchpoint onboarding sequence (Days 1, 3, 7, 30) achieves 70%+ 90-day retention. Templates for every email are inside the guide.

——

💰 STEP 5 — REVENUE MULTIPLIER
Subscriptions are your floor. Quarterly workshops ($199–$499), digital product drops, and referral programs stack 40–60% additional revenue on top — and turn your best members into your best marketers.

——

The framework is circular: Step 5 generates testimonials that feed Step 1. The community sells itself.

→ https://publixion.com/guides/build-paid-community-from-scratch

#PaidCommunity #RevenueFramework #RecurringRevenue #MRR #Publixion

posts._note: Call out specific types of people this guide is for. Be precise — specificity sells.
posts.linkedin:
Build a Paid Community from Scratch is not for everyone.

Here is precisely who it's designed for:

✅ YOU if:

→ You have an existing audience — email list, social following, or client base — of any size and want to convert that trust into recurring monthly revenue
→ You're a creator, coach, consultant, or expert who is tired of one-time launches and wants predictable MRR instead
→ You've thought about launching a community but don't know what to charge, which platform to use, or how to keep people from cancelling
→ You want scripts for every email, worksheets for every decision, and a calendar for every day of month one — not vague strategy
→ You're willing to commit to a weekly engagement rhythm and 90 days of consistent execution
→ You want a community that compounds — not one that flatlines after the launch buzz fades

❌ NOT YOU if:

→ You have zero existing audience or trust built with any group of people
→ You want to launch and then disappear — communities require ongoing presence
→ You expect $5,000 MRR in week one without following the framework
→ You want a purely passive asset with no member interaction

Your audience is already there. The Revenue Community Framework™ is the architecture that turns that attention into income.

$29.99. 90 days. $5,000+ MRR.

→ https://publixion.com/guides/build-paid-community-from-scratch

#PaidCommunity #RecurringRevenue #CreatorEconomy #CommunityBuilding #Publixion

posts._note: Show how a specific person would use this guide step by step. LinkedIn + Facebook only.
posts.linkedin:
Meet Creator A. Content strategist. 1,000 Instagram followers. No prior paid community. No email list.

Here's exactly what happened when she followed the Revenue Community Framework™:

——

📅 WEEK 1 — Positioning
Completed the Niche Clarity Worksheet. Chose: "Content strategy for solopreneurs going from $5K to $15K/month."
Built her one-sentence pitch: "Join [Community] to go from $5K to $15K/month in 60 days — even if you have no team."

📅 WEEK 2 — Architecture
Set 3 tiers: Free (newsletter) / Core ($15/month) / Premium ($49/month).
Mapped benefits to each tier using the Tier Benefit Mapping Table. Every benefit had a justified price.

📅 WEEK 3 — Platform + Onboarding
Chose Circle. Set up resource vault with 5 existing content pieces. Wrote Day 1, 3, 7, and 30 onboarding emails using the guide's copy templates. Set up automation. Total setup time: 6 hours.

📅 WEEK 4 — Soft Launch
Sent personal invites to 10 warm followers. Free tier first.
17 joined in 48 hours. 4 upgraded to Core within the first week.

📅 DAY 45
Thursday live Q&A running weekly without fail. First cohort challenge completed — 6 members shared wins publicly. New signups accelerated from social proof.

📅 DAY 60
First $5,000 MRR month: 200 paid members across Core + Premium.
71% 90-day retention. 6 inbound leads for 1-on-1 work from community members.

📅 DAY 75
Ran first quarterly workshop ($297). 28 attendees. $8,316 in a single day — separate from subscriptions.

——

She started with 1,000 followers and a system. That's all this takes.

→ https://publixion.com/guides/build-paid-community-from-scratch

#PaidCommunity #CaseStudy #RecurringRevenue #MRR #Publixion
posts.facebook:
Creator B. B2B marketing consultant. 10,000 email subscribers. 800-member free Discord community. Zero monetization.

He'd been giving everything away for free for 2 years. Here's what happened when he followed the Revenue Community Framework™:

Week 1: Completed Niche Clarity Worksheet. Repositioned from "marketing tips" to "B2B marketing for agency owners scaling to $50K/month." Wrote one-sentence community pitch. First time he'd ever defined his transformation that precisely.

Week 2: Rebuilt pricing architecture. Launched "Founding Member" rate of $9/month for first 100 members to reward loyalty and create urgency simultaneously.

Week 3: Deployed the Engagement Engine. Replaced ad-hoc posting with Mon–Fri structured rhythm. Engagement rate went from 4% → 23% in 8 weeks.

Day 30: 500 paid members. MRR: $11,200.

Day 60: Ran first quarterly workshop at $299. 47 attendees. $14,053 in a single day.

Day 90: Launched referral program using the guide's script. Top referrer that month brought 14 new members. Community is now self-marketing.

Month 4: Churn dropped 35% after rebuilding Core tier benefits based on exit survey data (template in the guide). LTV tripled.

He didn't start from nothing. He started from an audience that was already there — and finally gave it a reason to pay.

👉 https://publixion.com/guides/build-paid-community-from-scratch

posts._note: Handle the top doubts: price, time, skill level, whether it will work for them.
posts.linkedin:
"I want to launch a paid community — but I keep talking myself out of it."

Here are the objections I hear every week — and the direct answers:

——

❓ "$29.99 is a lot for a guide."

Your first 10 Core members at $15/month = $150 MRR. That pays for the guide 5× over — every single month. Your first Premium member at $49/month pays for it in week one. This is the lowest-risk $29.99 investment in your business.

——

❓ "I don't have a big enough audience."

Creator A in the case study started with 1,000 Instagram followers and hit 200 paid members and $5,000 MRR in 60 days. Audience size is not the variable. Positioning clarity and offer architecture are. The guide teaches both.

——

❓ "I've seen communities die in month 2. What stops that from happening?"

The Retention Loop — Day 1/3/7/30 onboarding sequence. 67% of community churn happens in the first 30 days because there is no structured onboarding. The guide gives you copy-paste emails for every touchpoint. Communities with this system achieve 70%+ 90-day retention.

——

❓ "I don't know what to charge or which platform to use."

The Tier Benefit Mapping Worksheet and Platform Comparison (Discord vs Circle vs Mighty Networks) are inside the guide. Every decision is mapped. You don't need to figure anything out from scratch.

——

❓ "What if I launch and nobody joins?"

The guide includes a soft launch protocol — 10 personal invitations to warm contacts before any public announcement. This generates the first members and social proof before you face a public audience.

——

You don't have a capability problem. You have an architecture problem. The guide solves it.

→ https://publixion.com/guides/build-paid-community-from-scratch

#PaidCommunity #RecurringRevenue #CommunityBuilding #Publixion
